summary: Public-facing pages /login, /signup, and /pricing contain zero hyperlink to /privacy-policy or /terms — a prospective user creating an account or subscribing to a paid plan has no in-context way to reach the Privacy Policy before signing up or paying.
reproduction: 1. In a fresh, unauthenticated browser session, navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login.
2. Inspect all <a> elements on the page (or visually scan for a footer/legal-links row).
3. Observe there is no link to /privacy-policy or /terms anywhere on the page.

6. Note https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/ itself performs a server redirect to /dashboard (which then bounces unauthenticated users to /login) — it is not a standalone marketing page with its own footer, so it was not separately assessed for a footer link.
expected: A signup/login/pricing flow for a product that collects personal data and takes payment conventionally links to its Privacy Policy and Terms (often required for informed-consent / checkbox-adjacent UX), and the sidebar footer inside the authenticated dashboard already does this (Privacy Policy · Terms · © 2026 Aether) — the same courtesy is expected pre-authentication.
observed: DOM link enumeration on /login (1 link total: 'Create account'), /signup (1 link total: 'Sign in'), and /pricing (2 links total: 'Sign in' + plan CTA anchors) shows no privacy-policy or terms href on any of the three pages. Confirmed live via Playwright twice in independent fresh browser sessions (session 1 and session 3).

summary: The Privacy Policy page contains zero reference to Australian privacy law or jurisdiction (no 'Privacy Act 1988', no 'Australian Privacy Principles'/APP, no 'ABN', not even the word 'Australia' appears anywhere on the page) despite the product being explicitly billed in AUD with GST to Australian consumers.
reproduction: 1. In a fresh, unauthenticated browser session, navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/privacy-policy.
2. Extract the full visible text of the <main> content region.
3. Search the text (case-insensitive) for 'Privacy Act', 'Australian Privacy', 'ABN', 'jurisdiction', and 'Australia' (any case).

4. Cross-check https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/pricing which states 'All prices are in Australian dollars and GST-inclusive' and https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/terms which states 'priced in Australian dollars (AUD)' and requires an 'Australian Business Number' for tax invoices — confirming this is an Australian-market product.
5. Observe none of the searched terms appear on the Privacy Policy page.
expected: For an Australian-market SaaS product handling personal data (resumes, Gmail content, billing metadata) and charging GST, a Privacy Policy conventionally names the applicable legal framework (Australian Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) / Australian Privacy Principles) so users know what recourse/regulator applies to them.
observed: Corrected live text-search (first-pass regex had a false positive on the common word 'app'; corrected check confirms zero matches) shows no mention of 'Privacy Act', 'Australian Privacy', 'ABN', 'jurisdiction', or 'Australia' anywhere in the 8 policy sections. Reproduced twice in independent fresh sessions.

summary: Section 5 ('Your Rights') and Section 7 ('Contact') direct users who want to exercise their data-export/account-deletion rights to 'contact us via the Settings page or the in-app support channel' — but neither actually provides a contact mechanism. dashboard/settings only re-states 'contact us to request a full data export or deletion' with no email, form, or address, and no support widget/help icon/mailto link/contact form exists anywhere in the live product.
reproduction: 1. Log in (canonical admin/admin123) and navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/privacy-policy.
2. Read Section 5 'Your Rights' > 'Data export or full account deletion': 'contact us (see Contact below) and we will process your request manually.'
3. Read Section 7 'Contact': 'you can reach us via the Settings page or the in-app support channel.' Neither 'Settings' nor 'in-app support channel' is a hyperlink (both are plain bold text).

4. Navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/settings and inspect the Privacy tab — it states the same 'contact us to request a full data export or deletion' with no actual channel provided (no email address, form, or link).
5. Visually inspect the full authenticated dashboard sidebar/header/settings for any help icon, chat widget, support link, or mailto: address — none exists.

6. Source-level confirmation: no 'mailto:', 'support@', or 'contact@' string exists anywhere in apps/web/src/app (excluding the two page files that merely reference the phrase), and no chat-widget/help-icon component exists in the dashboard layout or sidebar.
expected: A legal 'Your Rights' section that promises a path to exercise a data-subject right (export/deletion) should provide an actual, reachable contact method (email address, contact form, or support ticket flow) — not a circular reference to a page that itself just says 'contact us'.
observed: The promised contact path is a dead end: Settings > Privacy tab loops back to the same unfulfilled 'contact us' language, and no support channel (chat widget, help icon, contact form, or listed email address) exists anywhere in the authenticated app or public pages. A user cannot discover how to actually submit a data deletion/export request through the product.
